reference: Hi, I am Gravida three, Para two at twenty-seven weeks and five days. In my first trimester, the doctor noted that my placenta was posterior and low-lying. However, during the second trimester, I had at least another two ultrasound scans, and the doctors have said that the placenta was posterior and not low-lying anymore. Apparently, I have another scan today, and the doctor told me that I have Type three placenta previa. I am shocked and worried. Is it possible that this is a wrong diagnosis?
text: So, Nicki, you have a lot of options.
